title: selinum gmelinii
intro: Selinum gmelinii, also known as Russian angelica or gmelin's hog's fennel, is a perennial plant belonging to the carrot family that is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia and Siberia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant actions, making it effective in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and act as a natural diuretic, making it a popular choice in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Selinum gmelinii typically involve the roots and rhizomes being dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments.
